user.title: Wiener Linien Austria
user.description: Search for a stop by name.

data_description.search_query: Start typing and hit submit. Example: Stephansplatz

select_lines.title: Lines at {stop_name}
select_lines.description: Found {line_count} lines currently serving this stop. Pick the ones you want to track.

data_description.scan_interval: Minimum 30 seconds — twice the conventional 15-second floor circulated for the OGD endpoint, for safety margin under shared-IP scenarios.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure {stop_name}
reconfigure.description: Update the tracked lines or the polling interval. Entities are preserved.

error.catalogue_unavailable: Cannot load the Wiener Linien stop catalogue. Check your internet connection and retry.
error.invalid_stop: The selected stop is no longer available — search again.
error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the Wiener Linien real-time API. Try again in a minute.

abort.stop_gone: This stop is no longer in the Wiener Linien catalogue. Delete and re-add.
abort.catalogue_unavailable: Cannot load the Wiener Linien stop catalogue. Try again later.

init.title: Wiener Linien Austria options
init.description: Change the polling interval. Line selection is changed via Reconfigure.

api_timeout.message: Wiener Linien API timed out after {seconds} s

api_invalid_response.message: Wiener Linien API returned an unexpected response (status {status}): {error}

api_rate_limited.message: Wiener Linien API rate limit hit (error 316). Back off and retry.

api_upstream_error.message: Wiener Linien API reported error code {code}: {value}

rate_limited.title: Wiener Linien rate limit hit
rate_limited.description: Wiener Linien replied with error 316 (rate limit exceeded) to polls for **{entry_title}**. The conventional minimum interval circulated for the OGD endpoint is 15 s; this integration defaults to 60 s and enforces a 30 s floor, so normally this issue only appears when many HA instances share the same outbound IP. Raise the scan interval or reduce the number of concurrent entries.
